title: taraxacum dubium
intro: Taraxacum dubium, also known as the lesser dandelion, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its yellow flowers and jagged, toothed leaves. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum dubium lie in its roots, which contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can aid digestion and support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria. The health benefits of consuming Taraxacum dubium include reduced inflammation, improved liver function, and enhanced immune system response, making it a popular remedy for various digestive issues and skin problems. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum dubium can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
